title: AI Isn't My Cup of Tea
subtitle: LEO — The First Business Computer
premise: Every computer before LEO was built for war. Colossus broke Enigma. ENIAC aimed artillery. In 1951, a tea company in Hammersmith built the first computer used for business. This is our story.

style_guide.tone: warm, documentary, melancholic optimism — the weight of history made intimate
style_guide.rendering_notes: Fine cross-hatching for shadow. Clean linework. No digital gradients. Looks hand-drawn. Sepia wash over ink. Period-accurate architecture, clothing, machinery. No anachronisms.

narrator.name: The Robot
narrator.identity: The narrator is an avatar for computer intelligence — not LEO specifically. The broader voice of computing, speaking from the present, looking back at where it all started.
narrator.voice: Warm, wry, first-person. Never says 'AI' or labels itself. Just speaks.

characters.name: J.R.M. Simmons
characters.description: Systems comptroller at J. Lyons & Co. Methodical, patient, visionary. Wore glasses. Quiet conviction rather than charisma.
characters.visual_notes: Middle-aged British man, late 30s–50s across the story arc. Glasses. Conservative 1930s–1950s suit. Dark hair, neatly combed. Often seen at desks, in boardrooms, or alone with papers. Expression: watchful, never dramatic.

characters.name: Nippy
characters.description: A Lyons Corner House waitress. The human face of the empire. Precise, composed, professional.
characters.visual_notes: Black dress, white apron, white cap. Carries a tray. Moves with practised efficiency. 1930s London. Warm expression, but focused on her work.

characters.name: Checking Department Clerk
characters.description: One of three hundred women operating Burroughs adding machines in the Checking Department.
characters.visual_notes: 1930s–1940s office dress. Hair pinned up. Seated at a wooden desk partition with a Burroughs adding machine. Focused downward on her work. Represents all three hundred — not an individual.

characters.name: Office Boy
characters.description: Young male clerk who sorts the numbered leather bags from teashops each morning.
characters.visual_notes: Teenager or early 20s. Waistcoat, rolled sleeves. Working at a long wooden sorting table. Methodical.

recurring_props.name: Farthing coin
recurring_props.description: A single pre-decimal British farthing coin. Small, worn, copper.

recurring_props.name: Burroughs Adding Machine
recurring_props.description: A 1930s mechanical Burroughs adding machine. Black metal body, numbered keys, paper roll output.

recurring_props.name: Numbered Leather Bags
recurring_props.description: Locked leather satchels numbered by teashop. Arrive each morning from across the city.

recurring_props.name: Simmons's 1923 Notebook
recurring_props.description: An open notebook showing handwritten text dated 1923: 'The day will come when machines will do this work.'

recurring_props.name: 1947 Memo
recurring_props.description: A typed internal memo dated October 1947. Subject: study tour to American computing laboratories.

recurring_environments.name: Cadby Hall Exterior
recurring_environments.description: The vast Lyons headquarters and factory complex in Hammersmith, West London. Victorian industrial brick, iron gates, delivery lorries.
recurring_environments.lighting_notes: Overcast London sky. Morning fog. Diffuse grey light. Warm interior light glimpsed through windows.

recurring_environments.name: Corner House Dining Room
recurring_environments.description: A grand Lyons Corner House restaurant. Chandeliers, white tablecloths, wood panelling. Busy with ordinary Londoners.
recurring_environments.lighting_notes: Warm interior. Tungsten light. Evening or afternoon. Welcoming.

recurring_environments.name: Checking Department
recurring_environments.description: The vast clerical hall at Cadby Hall. Rows of wooden desk partitions extending to a vanishing point. Burroughs machines at every desk. Three hundred women working in near-silence.
recurring_environments.lighting_notes: Overhead industrial fluorescent. Flat, institutional. No warmth.

recurring_environments.name: Cadby Hall Food Factory
recurring_environments.description: Industrial cake and food production. Conveyor belts, baking machinery, steel and steam. One worker supervising many machines.
recurring_environments.lighting_notes: Industrial. Steam and heat. Bright overhead.

recurring_environments.name: Lyons Boardroom
recurring_environments.description: A formal 1930s boardroom. Wood-panelled walls, long table, suited directors. Charts on a stand at one end.
recurring_environments.lighting_notes: Natural window light from one side. Formal, slightly tense.

recurring_environments.name: Simmons's Office — Evening
recurring_environments.description: A modest private office. Wooden desk stacked with papers. Single desk lamp. London rooftops visible through the window at night.
recurring_environments.lighting_notes: Single warm tungsten desk lamp. Everything else in shadow. Window shows dark city.
